steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says 'no amount of justification... is likely to persuade' these drivers. The blank must describe the toll in a way that explains why no argument can move them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — warranted: Means justified or deserved — the opposite of what drivers believe. A toll they consider warranted would not generate unyielding resistance. ✗
  • Choice B — exorbitant: Means unreasonably, excessively high. If drivers already view the toll as far too much, no further justification for raising it could persuade them — this fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — equivocal: Means ambiguous or open to more than one interpretation. The passage describes a firm objection, not confusion about the amount. ✗
  • Choice D — contentious: Means causing disagreement. It is too vague — it says the toll is disputed but not that drivers believe it is already too high. ✗
That is the correct answer.
